cassette: none: reducer.PostgresIncidentRepositoryCorrelationWriter operates over []IncidentRepositoryCorrelationDecision Go values, not a CanonicalMaterialization; the two fixture decisions live inline in go/internal/replay/costcounting/incident_repository_correlation_cost_test.go
description: Two exact-outcome incident-repository decisions for distinct provider services in one scope, driven through the production Postgres fact writer over an InstrumentedDB-wrapped counting fake. WriteIncidentRepositoryCorrelations now calls the shared reducerBatchInsertFacts bounded chunked bulk insert (go/internal/reducer/reducer_fact_batch_insert.go, batch size 1000, issue #5317) instead of one ExecContext per decision: two rows fit one chunk, so eshu_dp_postgres_query_duration_seconds records exactly 1 write observation and statements_executed=1. Any increase — an N+1 write-per-decision regression — trips the gate; the companion N+1 negative control test proves this by calling the writer once per decision and asserting the count exceeds this budget.
refresh_path: manual: edit the inline fixture decisions in incident_repository_correlation_cost_test.go and this file together, reviewed in the same diff (no credentialed cassette refresh applies since there is no cassette)
